name: BRDGIT — Slack Topics to Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## BRDGIT — Slack Topics to Google Sheets

**Three flows in this workflow.** All feed the same Topics tab in the BRDGIT topics sheet.

### 1) Hourly Ingestion from Slack (top row)
- Hourly Trigger fires at the top of every hour
- Reads existing topics from the sheet (for dedup, branched off the trigger)
- Pulls Jarvis's recent messages from #ai-roundup (last 2 hours)
- Filters to Jarvis-authored, cleans Slack formatting
- Skips any topic already in the sheet (case-insensitive)
- Appends new topics to the Topics tab

**Why polling instead of Slack Trigger:**
The Slack Trigger has limitations with bot-authored messages and didn't reliably fire for Jarvis posts. Polling channel:history is bulletproof — works for bot, webhook, app, and human messages alike.

### 2) Daily Cleanup (middle row)
Runs at 2am. Removes topics that are unused (blank Last Used) AND have a Date Added older than 7 days. Keeps the sheet trimmed so stale ideas don't linger.

### 3) AI Topic Generator (bottom row, every 3 days at 9am)
Backup topic source for when Slack #ai-roundup is quiet. Runs on a 72-hour interval.

- Schedule Trigger fires every 3 days at 9am
- Reads existing topics from the sheet (for dedup, branched off the trigger)
- **AI Topic Agent** (Claude Sonnet 4.5) generates 5–10 newsletter-worthy topic hooks at the intersection of software development + AI consulting
- Agent calls the `Search in Tavily (AI Gen)` tool 2–4 times to ground topics in current trends (recent announcements, framework releases, surprising data, shifts in how engineering teams work)
- Prompt bans generic takes ("AI is transforming everything", "5 ways AI helps developers") and demands specificity + a defendable angle
- **Parse & Dedupe AI Topics** parses the JSON output, drops anything that's already in the sheet (case-insensitive), and outputs new topics tagged with `source: 'AI Generator'`
- Appends new topics to the Topics tab

Topics from this flow go through the same downstream pipeline as Slack-sourced ones — picked up by the Organic Content Automation workflow on Mon/Wed/Fri at 9am, and aged out by the Daily Cleanup if they go unused for 7 days.

**Bot requirements (for the Slack ingestion flow):**
- The n8n Slack bot user must be invited to #ai-roundup
- Slack app needs `channels:history`, `groups:history`, `mpim:history`, `im:history` scopes

**Credentials reused:**
- Google Sheets BRDGIT
- Slack n8n Bot
- Anthropic account 10 (Claude Sonnet 4.5)
- Tavily account 2

**Sheet:** Topics tab in `YOUR_GOOGLE_SPREADSHEET_ID`
Columns: `Topic | Last Used | Date Added`

nodes.name: Get Existing Topics for AI Gen

parameters.text:
=You are a content strategist for BRDGIT, an AI consulting firm. Your job is to surface 5–10 newsletter-worthy topic ideas that BRDGIT's marketing team will turn into LinkedIn thought-leadership posts.

Focus area: the intersection of SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT and AI CONSULTING. Good topics include:
- AI tooling in dev workflows (Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, agentic coding patterns)
- LLM-powered devtools and how teams are actually using them
- AI adoption inside engineering orgs (org design, skills, hiring, process)
- The economics and ROI of AI in software teams
- Practical AI consulting case studies and recurring patterns
- Industry shifts (new frameworks, model releases, regulations) and what they mean for dev teams
- Where AI consulting is going as the technology and the market mature

What makes a topic GOOD:
- SPECIFIC: "How agentic coding is reshaping code review" beats "AI in software development"
- HAS AN ANGLE: a point of view worth defending, not a neutral overview
- NEWSWORTHY OR CURRENT: tied to something happening now, not evergreen platitudes
- NOT OBVIOUS: avoid takes everyone has already written

What makes a topic BAD (do NOT generate these):
- "AI is transforming everything"
- "The future of software development"
- "Why companies need AI"
- Generic listicles ("5 ways AI helps developers")
- Anything that could have been written two years ago

RESEARCH (required):
You have access to the `Search in Tavily (AI Gen)` tool. Call it 2–4 times to find what is actually being discussed RIGHT NOW in software dev + AI consulting. Diversify your queries — don't search for the same theme repeatedly. Useful angles to search for:
- Recent announcements (new tools, model releases, framework launches)
- Surprising data or studies about AI adoption in engineering
- Controversy or healthy disagreement in the dev community
- Shifts in how engineering teams operate or hire

Synthesize what you learn into topics that feel current and earned, not generic.

OUTPUT:
Return ONLY valid JSON with this exact shape (no markdown, no code fences, just raw JSON):
{
  "topics": [
    "First topic as a complete hook or sentence, 8–20 words",
    "Second topic…",
    "…"
  ]
}

Generate between 5 and 10 topics. Each should stand on its own as a possible LinkedIn post hook.

main.node: Get Existing Topics for AI Gen
